data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-60bc5e9a">
    <title>feat(escalation): implement DECOMPOSE path with decomposer.md template and agent dispatch</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (ddx-e012af90) routes TooLarge
  failure class to a DECOMPOSE path, but no decomposer prompt template and no
  decomposer dispatch logic exist. When the hook fires for TooLarge (or as a
  fallback from reframe failure on PersistentExecutionFailed), it must dispatch
  a strong-tier agent to produce 2-5 child bead specs, then file them via
  Store.Create with the parent linked.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has PostAttemptDecompositionHook for
  the no_changes/orchestrator_action:decompose path, but no general decomposer
  for post-ladder-exhaustion scenarios.
  library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md does not exist.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md defining:
     - Input contract: original bead body and failure history (same as reframer).
     - Output contract: JSON array of 2-5 child specs, each with: title (string),
       description (string), acceptance (string), labels ([]string).
     - Role guidance: each child must be independently executable, ~3-6 ACs,
       with explicit deps if ordering required.
  2. Add decomposer dispatch in cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:
     - runDecomposer(ctx, store, executor, beadID, failureHistory) → DecomposeResult
     - Dispatches a smart+ tier agent.
     - Parses JSON array output; validates 1-5 children, each passes intake
       validation (non-empty title, description, acceptance).
     - Creates children via store.Create with Parent=beadID.
     - Sets execution-eligible=false on parent via bead.Extra key.
     - Emits decompose-applied event with child IDs and costUSD.
  3. Failure modes explicitly handled (each returns DecomposeResult{failed:true}):
     - Timeout exceeded.
     - Empty output.
     - <1 or >5 children in parsed result.
     - Any child spec failing intake validation (empty title, description, or AC).
  4. Hook (from ddx-e012af90) invokes runDecomposer for TooLarge class; on success
     returns PostLadderExhaustionResult{attempted:true, succeeded:true, path:Decompose}.

NON-SCOPE
  Reframe path (child bead 2). Circuit breaker / escape hatch (child bead 4).
  PersistentExecutionFailed → decompose fallback is wired in child bead 4.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md exists with: (a) input contract naming bead body + failure history; (b) output contract specifying a JSON array of 2-5 objects each with title, description, acceptance, labels fields; (c) role guidance requiring each child be independently executable with ~3-6 ACs.
2. cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go exists and defines runDecomposer() dispatching a smart+ agent, parsing output, validating children, and creating them via store.Create.
3. Decompose success: 2-5 child beads created with Parent=beadID; parent's Extra has execution-eligible=false; decompose-applied event emitted with child IDs and costUSD.
4. Failure mode: timeout → DecomposeResult{failed:true, reason:"timeout"}.
5. Failure mode: empty output → DecomposeResult{failed:true, reason:"empty_output"}.
6. Failure mode: <1 or >5 children in parsed array → DecomposeResult{failed:true, reason:"invalid_count"}.
7. Failure mode: any child with empty title, description, or acceptance → DecomposeResult{failed:true, reason:"malformed_child"}.
8. PostLadderExhaustionHook (ddx-e012af90) invokes runDecomposer for TooLarge class; returns PostLadderExhaustionResult{attempted:true, succeeded:true/false} accordingly.
9. TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge passes: verifies TooLarge failure class routes to decomposer and, on valid children, creates child beads and sets parent execution-eligible=false.
10. TestDecomposerInvalidChildren_CountsAsFailure passes: stub agent returns >5 children; verifies DecomposeResult.failed=true with reason="invalid_count".
11. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-63155d5c" base-rev="0e29a74fcca192d514b8dbede233e57ea4b759d0" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T030309-64d604ac"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All three files are in HEAD — the DDx checkpoint at `fde206bdd` captured the Go files before my commit, and my commit added the `.md` file. The working tree is clean. All ACs are satisfied:

- **AC1**: `library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md` — input contract (bead body + failure history), output contract (JSON array of 2-5 objects with title/description/acceptance/labels), role guidance (3-6 ACs, independently executable)
- **AC2**: `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go` — `runDecomposer()` dispatches strong-tier agent, parses output, validates, creates children via `store.Create`
- **AC3**: Children created with `Parent=beadID`, parent `Extra[execution-eligible]=false`, `decompose-applied` event with child_ids and cost_usd
- **AC4-7**: Failure modes — timeout → `"timeout"`, empty output → `"empty_output"`, invalid count → `"invalid_count"`, malformed child → `"malformed_child"`
- **AC8**: `NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook` handles TooLarge only, returns `PostLadderExhaustionResult{Attempted:true, Succeeded:true/false, Path:Decompose}`
- **AC9**: `TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge` passes
- **AC10**: `TestDecomposerInvalidChildren_CountsAsFailure` passes
- **AC11**: `go test ./internal/agent/...` green
- **AC12**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes
